message:
fix(distributed): widen ESI acceptance range for high-loss recovery scenarios

recovery.rs:
- Increase the ESI range guard from max_expected + 100 to max_expected + 50_000
  in add_collected_with_verify. The previous +100 buffer was too tight for
  high-loss network scenarios where receivers legitimately need thousands of
  repair symbols beyond the source block count. RaptorQ supports up to ~16M
  ESIs, so 50k repair symbols is a safe upper bound that still catches obvious
  garbage (random/corrupt ESI values) while accommodating realistic repair
  overhead in degraded network conditions

bridge.rs:
- Add TODO(Phase 4) comment in apply_snapshot documenting that the current
  implementation only acknowledges the snapshot sequence number, making the
  bridge effectively write-only. Recovery and follower replica use cases will
  need full local state application

tests/repro_recovery_high_esi.rs:
- New integration test that creates a symbol with ESI 200 (K=10, old limit was
  110) and verifies it is NOT rejected as CorruptedSymbol. The test provides
  9 low-ESI source symbols plus 1 high-ESI repair symbol and confirms that
  any failure is a decoding error (expected with zero-filled test data), not
  a range-check rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
